blockers.statement: Execution tasks DMC-T012a-e collide in prefix with master plan s7's DMC-T012 (worker-result-check hardening, an unrelated task at dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md line 309) and do not match the sub-plan's own master task ID (master s6.5's task is DMC-T011b, line 288). Handoff rev 4 carry-forward #8 records this renumber is to be applied 'at the M6/M6.5 critic passes,' and the M6 plan already set the binding precedent by renumbering its tasks to DMC-T011.1-.4 (dmc-v1-m6-hook-hardening.md lines 16, 298) to avoid exactly this collision. The M6.5 plan has not applied the equivalent renumber, so it is inconsistent with the precedent this critic lane must enforce. FIX: renumber DMC-T012a-e -> DMC-T011b.1 .. DMC-T011b.5 (sub-numbering under master s6.5's task DMC-T011b), following the M6 precedent; verified clean (grep confirms DMC-T011b.N is currently unused anywhere in .harness/ or docs/). Update handoff carry-forward #8 to reflect the applied rename. This removes the T012 collision and aligns the sub-plan to its own master task ID.
blockers.evidence_ref: .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m6.5-codex-adapter.md Execution Tasks; .harness/plans/dmc-v1-runtime-upgrade.md L288,L309; .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m6-hook-hardening.md L16,L298; handoff carry-forward #8

blockers.statement: Acceptance criterion 2 enumerates only well-formed happy-path fixtures (in-scope->allow, out-of-scope edit->deny, secret-path read->deny, out-of-scope post-Bash diff->BLOCKED, stop-with-unresolved-BLOCKED->block, suspended-run->no block). It specifies no behavior or fixture for degenerate/error inputs: unparseable or empty Codex event JSON; a missing or renamed tool_input field (field names are explicitly TBD-at-spike per CODEX_ADAPTER s2/s3); a Ring-0 verdict CLI that exits non-zero/unexpectedly or is absent; an absent .harness/mode file (CLAUDE.md: absent => active). 'Fail-closed in active mode' is asserted in prose only. Because the cross-adapter verdict-parity guard binds only inputs present in both fixture suites, and every enumerated fixture is well-formed, parity does NOT cover fail-closed behavior - a Codex shim could fail-open on malformed input (or a wrong field name) and still satisfy every listed criterion. For an enforcement-class shim this is the load-bearing gap. FIX: add a per-bound-event negative-control fixture set proving fail-closed on each of (a) unparseable/empty event JSON, (b) missing/renamed expected field, (c) Ring-0 CLI non-zero/unexpected exit or absent binary, (d) absent .harness/mode; each asserting a deny/block envelope in active mode, and extend the cross-adapter parity check to include these malformed inputs.
blockers.evidence_ref: .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m6.5-codex-adapter.md Acceptance Criteria (criterion 2) + Proposed Changes (shim change); docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md s2 (tool-input key names TBD at spike), s3 (field-name shim pending)

blockers.statement: Codex event JSON on stdin carries tool_input, which for edit tools can include file paths AND file content and for Bash the full command string - any of which may contain secret material (keys, tokens, .env content). The plan routes this JSON through the shims and (CODEX_ADAPTER s2) into the PostToolUse evidence/receipt ledger, but no acceptance criterion or negative control requires that raw secret-bearing tool_input is never persisted or logged. This violates CLAUDE.md's non-negotiable secret-protection rule as applied to the new enforcement-adjacent surface. The Claude hooks already implement redaction (confirmed: .claude/hooks/evidence-log.sh and pre-tool-guard.sh carry redaction/Authorization handling); the Codex side is not bound to that standard, and verdict-parity does not cover it because logging is not a verdict. FIX: add an acceptance criterion + negative control that feeds an edit/Bash event whose tool_input contains a secret-looking payload and asserts that no raw secret content appears in any evidence/receipt/log output from the Codex shim; bind the Codex shims explicitly to the existing redaction contract.
blockers.evidence_ref: .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m6.5-codex-adapter.md Acceptance Criteria; docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md s2; .claude/hooks/evidence-log.sh, .claude/hooks/pre-tool-guard.sh; CLAUDE.md Secret Protection

blockers.statement: T012a's success requires empirically re-proving that 'hook events fire' and 'deny/allow/block envelopes [are] honored,' but the plan constrains the spike to run 'offline-where-possible ... must not require or read any API key ... no DMC-initiated network call' (Out of Scope). Proving a PreToolUse deny/allow/block envelope is honored at runtime requires the Codex agent to attempt a tool call - i.e., a live model turn requiring CODEX_API_KEY + network - which the constraint forbids. As written, the two most load-bearing airtightness facts may be unprovable under the spike's own rules, which would silently force the documented-manual fallback for exactly the facts the shims depend on, without the plan stating that outcome. An implementer cannot tell whether to (i) find a turn-free proof method, (ii) request a human-approved scoped live turn, or (iii) treat these facts as fallback-triggering by default; and the plan maps only one refutation case (PostToolUse/unified_exec, Assumption row 3) rather than tabulating, per load-bearing s1 fact, whether refutation-or-unprovable-offline triggers a FULL milestone stop vs a scoped degradation. FIX: resolve the contradiction explicitly - specify a turn-free method to prove hook firing/envelope honoring (making 'does such a method exist' an explicit spike sub-question), OR carve a narrowly-scoped, human-approved, secret-free local turn as a stated exception, OR declare that if firing/envelope cannot be proven without a live turn the milestone takes the documented-manual + pre-commit/CI fallback by default; and add a per-fact table mapping each load-bearing s1 fact to full-stop vs scoped-degradation on refutation/unprovability.
blockers.evidence_ref: .harness/plans/dmc-v1-m6.5-codex-adapter.md Proposed Changes (SPIKE FIRST) + Out of Scope + Assumptions row 3 + T012a Notes; docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md s1 (hook enforcement honesty) + Open questions
